ogTitle: Glenelg North vs Glengowrie: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Glenelg North and Glengowrie.
heading: Glenelg North vs Glengowrie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Glenelg North, South Australia and Glengowrie, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Glengowrie has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Glenelg North (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Glenelg North or Glengowrie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Glenelg North has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Glengowrie scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Glenelg North scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Glengowrie scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Glenelg North or Glengowrie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Glengowrie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
